name: Harsh Kumar
education: Bachelor of Technology - BTech, Computer Science from Indian Institute of Technology - Banaras Hindu University (IIT-BHU), Varanasi
headline: Founder, CodingRippler | Helped 20+ businesses build the tech they needed
about:
I’ve seen how high-quality engineering works at scale, and how rarely that discipline reaches early-stage founders.

After building engineering systems at Microsoft, I started CodingRippler with a simple goal,
help founders take products from idea to live without overhiring, overengineering, or getting stuck in endless iterations.

We work with founders who want to ship, not just plan.

That usually means

→ Turning ideas or early concepts into live products
→ Rebuilding fragile systems so teams can move faster
→ Automating workflows that are slowing the business down

What we build

→ Web and mobile products
→ Backend systems, APIs, and integrations
→ Internal tools, dashboards, and automation

How we work

We operate as an extension of your team.
No junior handoffs. No disappearing acts. No vague timelines.

Just clear ownership, senior execution, and products that make it to production.

If you’re a founder looking to take something live, or get unstuck, happy to exchange notes.

currentPositions.description:
Currently contributing to the Microsoft Viva Connections team, building internal tools that enhance employee engagement and communication.

Led the end-to-end development of an internal social network feature, enabling users to post, share, and consume organizational news.

Designed and implemented reusable UI components from scratch, ensuring seamless integration across Viva Connections.

Collaborated with teams to integrate the 3SR architecture to support personalized re-ranking of news posts based on user preferences and engagement signals.

Played a key role in improving information discovery and relevance across internal communications within the organization.

experiences.title: Founder
experiences.jobDescription:
Building CodingRippler with a focus on helping founders take products from idea to live, without overhiring or overengineering.

→ Working directly with founders on product and technical decisions
→ Leading hands-on execution across web, backend, and automation
→ Taking products to production and supporting early real-world usage
→ Building systems that scale and are practical to operate
→ Acting as an execution partner, not a maintenance vendor

experiences.jobDescription:
Currently contributing to the Microsoft Viva Connections team, building internal tools that enhance employee engagement and communication.

Led the end-to-end development of an internal social network feature, enabling users to post, share, and consume organizational news.

Designed and implemented reusable UI components from scratch, ensuring seamless integration across Viva Connections.

experiences.title: AI Researcher
experiences.jobDescription:
1. Spearheaded initiatives focused on building Responsible AI systems to ensure fairness and compliance in deep learning models.
2. Conducted deep research and analysis of existing model architectures to identify sources of bias.
3. Proposed a novel architecture to mitigate algorithmic bias, tailored for real-world financial use cases.
4. Successfully developed a solution that was patented by Mastercard, reinforcing the organization’s leadership in ethical AI.
5. Contributed to shaping internal AI governance strategies and advancing Mastercard’s Responsible AI framework.

summary: Harsh Kumar is currently the founder and leader at CodingRippler, an India‑based startup/agency that helps founders and SMBs ship products, automate operations, and run AI-driven workflows. He consistently posts about practical automation and orchestration (especially using n8n/Make/Zapier), AI applications including LLMs and Voice AI, product and tech strategy for founders, and finance/process automation use cases. His posting style blends technical depth and hands‑on implementation guidance with founder-focused thought leadership—direct, pragmatic, and solution-oriented, often including concrete architectures and strong CTAs (DMs, pilots, case studies). He repeatedly emphasizes reliability and repeatability over novelty, warning about inconsistent AI outputs, promoting scenario‑based instructions, and stressing planning for post‑launch change. Posts routinely surface client outcomes, step‑by‑step approaches (e.g., Gmail → AI extraction → n8n → Sheets), and operational metrics, showing a bias toward fast, predictable delivery and accountable agency partnerships. Harsh differentiates himself by combining senior engineering execution with no‑code/low‑code orchestration, positioning Indian technical talent as a cost‑effective route to enterprise‑grade automation and by inviting community engagement to diagnose founder problems directly.
